hook.headline: Claude Skills turn one good prompt into a real, reusable agent.
hook.sub: no SDK · no infra · no glue code

cards.label: 01 · what
cards.title: A Skill is a folder.
cards.body: A skill is just a directory with a SKILL.md, supporting files, and optional scripts. Claude reads the SKILL.md, follows the instructions, calls the scripts when it needs them. That's it.

cards.label: 02 · why
cards.title: Prompts evaporate. Skills compound.
cards.body: Your best 800-word prompt only lives in your chat history. The same workflow as a Skill is a file you commit, reuse, share with your team, and improve over time.
cards.metric: → one Skill = one repeatable workflow forever

cards.label: 03 · stack
cards.title: Composes with MCP.
cards.body: A Skill can call MCP tools the same way Claude does — your CRM, your repo, your Notion. Skills are the recipe; MCP servers are the ingredients.
cards.metric: → skill + MCP = full agent in <50 LOC

cards.label: 04 · how
cards.title: Install in 30 seconds.
cards.body: Drop a folder into ~/.claude/skills/, restart Claude Code or the desktop app, and Claude can use it. No build step, no install command, no auth dance.

cards.label: 05 · example
cards.title: Real one: weekly LinkedIn post.
cards.body: SKILL.md tells Claude: pull last week's commits via the git MCP, draft a post in my voice, save to drafts/. Run /skill linkedin every Friday. That's a 3-step automation, zero framework.

take.use_if: You repeat the same long prompt more than 3 times — a Skill replaces it forever.
take.skip_if: You're a one-shot prompter and don't have repeatable workflows yet — start with MCP first.
